passages.passage_text: Directions (Q. Nos. 1- 44) Each of the following questions below consists of a question and two statements numbered I and II given below it. You have to decide whether the data provided in the statements are sufficient to answer the question. Give answer (a) if the data in Statement I alone are sufficient to answer the question, while the data in Statement II alone are not sufficient to answer the question (b) if the data in Statement II alone are sufficient to answer the question, while the data in Statement I alone are not sufficient to answer the question (c) if the data either in Statement I alone or Statement II alone are sufficient to answer the question (d) if the data in both the Statements I and II together are not sufficient to answer the question (e) if the data in both the Statements I and II are together necessary to answer the question

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: How will 'walk' be written in a certain code? I. In this code 'morning walk is good' is written as 'na pa ta sa'. II. In this code 'wish you good morning' is written as 'la na sa da'.

choices.a: if the data in Statement I alone are sufficient to answer the question, while the data in Statement II alone are not sufficient to answer the question
choices.b: if the data in Statement II alone are sufficient to answer the question, while the data in Statement I alone are not sufficient to answer the question
choices.c: if the data either in Statement I alone or Statement II alone are sufficient to answer the question
choices.d: if the data in both the Statements I and II together are not sufficient to answer the question
choices.e: if the data in both the Statements I and II are together necessary to answer the question

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: What is the code of 'market'? I. 'do not go' is coded as 'ka ma tok'. II. 'go to market' is code as 'ma jo la'.

choices.a: if the data in Statement I alone are sufficient to answer the question, while the data in Statement II alone are not sufficient to answer the question
choices.b: if the data in Statement II alone are sufficient to answer the question, while the data in Statement I alone are not sufficient to answer the question

choices.d: if the data in both the Statements I and II together are not sufficient to answer the question
choices.e: if the data in both the Statements I and II are together necessary to answer the question

questions.subject: verbal

questions.body: How will 'must' be written in a certain code? I. In that code language, 'you must see' is written as 'la pa ni' and 'you see' is written as 'jo ni pa'. II. In that code language 'you did that' is written as 'pa si jo'.
